chapter_context.summary: Presents the riddle of two great eagles, a cedar tree, and a vine, representing the rebellion of the vassal King of Judah against Babylon by seeking Egypt's aid, concluding with the promise of a tender twig planted by God on a high mountain.

outline.text: The Riddle of Two Eagles, the Cedar Top, and the Vine
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Riddle of Two Eagles, the Cedar Top, and the Vine (17:1-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Interpretation: Zedekiah's Rebellion Against Babylon and Egypt Alliance
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Interpretation: Zedekiah's Rebellion Against Babylon and Egypt Alliance (17:11-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: The High Mountain Twig: Prophesying a Messianic Restoration
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The High Mountain Twig: Prophesying a Messianic Restoration (17:22-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies Against Judah and Jerusalem
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies against judah and jerusalem, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 17, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
